tools.name: memory_store
tools.description: You forget EVERYTHING between sessions. If you discover something worth knowing later — a pattern, a decision, a bug fix, a user preference — store it NOW or it's gone forever. Only required field: a short description of what you learned.

description.description: What did you learn? Be specific — this is all you'll see in search results later

category.description: Domain tag for filtering. Examples: 'notes', 'personal preference', 'hardware', 'coding style', 'architecture'. Defaults to 'notes' if omitted.

confidence.description: How sure are you? 0-1 scale (default 0.5). 1.0 = verified fact, 0.0 = wild guess

data.description: Extended details (only visible via memory_get, hidden from listings). Use for context that would clutter search results.

tools.name: memory_recall
tools.description: CALL THIS FIRST at every session start — you have accumulated knowledge from past sessions that you're about to duplicate or contradict. Also use before implementation to check for prior solutions, or when stuck to find forgotten answers.

query.description: What are you looking for? Natural language — semantic matching finds related memories even with different wording

category.description: Filter to a specific domain tag

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Retrieve a single memory's full content including the data payload. Use when memory_recall returns a result with [has data] tag and you need the details.

tools.name: memory_forget
tools.description: Delete a memory by ID. Use when you find outdated, incorrect, or superseded memories during recall or review.

tools.name: memory_list
tools.description: Browse all stored memories or filter by category. Use for periodic review, cleanup sessions, or curiosity about what you already know.

category.description: Filter to a specific domain tag

tools.name: memory_update
tools.description: Update an existing memory when your understanding improves. Re-embeds automatically if text changes. Prefer over delete+store to preserve ID history.
